commandsRun: Get-FileHash -Algorithm SHA256 on artifact, packet, capsule, image-prompts.json, selections.json
ConvertFrom-Json parse of scripts/image-prompts.json (94 images; 87 menu-item + 7 marketing; required=1 brand-logo; reference_status generate-from-scratch=94)
ConvertFrom-Json parse of scripts/selections.json (94 variant selections; 0 missing source/provenance; slot-id alignment 94/94)
System.IO.File::Exists UTF-8-aware existence check over all 94 manifest output_file targets (0 missing) + 10 named-asset spot-check (all exist)
ls -b public/images/menu/family-meals-and-combos (confirmed em-dash U+2014 combo-dinner filenames on disk)
node higgsfield-workflow.mjs verify --project D:/website/captains-fish-and-chicken -> ok:true, problems:[], deferred slots/pages []
node scratchpad/sweep.mjs -> dist sweep: 38 pages, 94 unique refs, 0 broken, 138 img, 0 missing alt, 5/5 hero webp, 0 non-modern
grep representational across dist/*.html (present on 14 menu category pages + catering/home/open-late)
grep alt qualifier on dist/menu/seafood/index.html (menu-item alts end with '(representational image)')
ConvertFrom-Json parse of src/data/menu.json (frozen menu = 87 items across 14 categories; per-category counts match manifest 1:1)
Get-FileHash -Algorithm SHA256 on imagery-quality-review-notes.md (final notes bytes)
filesRead: D:\website\captains-fish-and-chicken\public\ (manifest export targets + named assets, existence checks)
assumptions: The sealed Phase 6 artifact named in the capsule (hash d2e25f43...) is the authoritative review target; it verified byte-for-byte, so the review proceeded.

packetHash and capsuleHash fields carry the contract-declared values from the capsule allowedInputs / orchestrator brief. The actual on-disk files hash differently (packet 9c9b2ed0..., capsule 7c2a8a1e...); see advisory A1 and the verification array. This mismatch does not affect the imagery-quality verdict because every gate finding was re-derived from primary sources, not from packet evidence.
'Every export EXISTS' was evaluated with a UTF-8-aware .NET existence check; a naive WinPS 5.1 Test-Path false-flagged two em-dash filenames that are in fact present on disk.

verification.check: manifest image-prompts.json sha256 vs artifact claim

verification.expected: 94 / 94 / 94, 87 menu-item + 7 marketing, every selection has source+provenance, every export on disk
verification.computed: 94 images (87 menu-item + 7 marketing, required=1), 94 variant selections (0 missing source/provenance), slot-id alignment 94/94, 94/94 exports exist on disk (UTF-8-aware), 10/10 named-asset spot-check exist

verification.expected: 0 broken refs, all heroes webp/avif, all img have alt
verification.computed: 38 pages, 94 unique refs, 0 broken, 138 img tags, 0 missing alt, 5/5 hero webp, 0 non-modern hero

verification.expected: note on menu pages, no generated-as-real-premises, logo placeholder acknowledged not silent
verification.computed: 'Photos are representational' on 14 menu category pages; menu-item alts end '(representational image)'; manifest blocker_notes label each render REPRESENTATIONAL; logo.png placeholder acknowledged non-blocking

verification.expected: desktop+mobile uiChecks present and passing, unresolvedIssues empty
verification.computed: status pass/ok true; desktop-ui-check pass; mobile-ui-check pass; unresolvedIssues [] empty; 3 advisories correctly routed forward

verification.check: criterion-e no fabrication vs frozen menu
verification.expected: 87 items, no off-menu images (e.g. no calamari)
verification.computed: src/data/menu.json = 87 items / 14 categories; manifest menu-item = 87; per-category counts match 1:1; no calamari/squid/lobster/oyster/scallop ids
